definition:
1. to strip  or despoil a slain enemy  of his arms, (Hesiod); with accusative of persons or things, Κύκνον σκυλεύσαντες ἀπp ὤμων τεύχεα having stript  the arms of Cycnus from his shoulders, (Hesiod) 
2. with accusative rei et genitive pers. to strip  the arms off  an enemy, (Xenophon Historicus); so, ἀπὸ τῶν νεκρῶν σκ. ψέλια (Herdotus Historicus) (ML)
